name: MemPalace
description: Local-first AI memory system that stores verbatim conversations in ChromaDB, organized into a navigable palace structure (wings, rooms, halls, tunnels) with a temporal knowledge graph and semantic search. No API keys required.
tagline: Verbatim local memory palace for AI
approachDetail: 4-layer memory stack: L0 identity (~50 tokens), L1 critical facts (~120 tokens), L2 on-demand wing/room retrieval, L3 deep semantic search. Conversations stored verbatim in ChromaDB, organized into wings (people/projects), rooms (topics), halls (memory types), and tunnels (cross-wing connections). Separate SQLite knowledge graph stores temporal entity-relationship triples.
searchDetail: ChromaDB vector similarity search with default embedding model. Supports wing/room metadata filtering for scoped queries. Palace structure reportedly improves retrieval by ~34% over unfiltered search.
storageDetail: ChromaDB PersistentClient for vector storage of verbatim content. SQLite for temporal knowledge graph. All data local under ~/.mempalace/.
mcpDetail: Full MCP server with 19 tools covering read, write, knowledge graph, navigation, and agent diary operations. Launched via python -m mempalace.mcp_server.
toolSupportDetail: Dedicated Claude Code plugin (marketplace), MCP server for any compatible tool, Gemini CLI integration guide, Codex plugin directory
scopesDetail: Wings provide per-project/per-person scoping. Global search across all wings supported via --wing flag.
duplicateDetail: mempalace_check_duplicate MCP tool with configurable similarity threshold (default 0.9). SHA-256 deterministic IDs for idempotency.
retentionDetail: No automatic TTL/expiry. Manual deletion via mempalace_delete_drawer. Knowledge graph facts can be invalidated with mempalace_kg_invalidate (sets end date, keeps history).
crossMachineDetail: Local ChromaDB + SQLite storage. No sync or replication.
tokenBudgetingDetail: 4-layer stack: L0+L1 wake-up is ~170-900 tokens, loaded automatically. L2/L3 load on demand. Layer1 capped at MAX_CHARS=3200 (~800 tokens), MAX_DRAWERS=15.
contextInjectionDetail: MCP server auto-teaches memory protocol on first status call. Claude Code hooks auto-trigger saves every 15 messages and before context compression. wake-up command generates L0+L1 context.
networkDetail: Fully local. ChromaDB with default embedding model, SQLite knowledge graph. No external API calls.
auditTrailDetail: Write-ahead log (WAL) at ~/.mempalace/wal/write_log.jsonl records all mutations with timestamps. File permissions set to 0o600.
pros: Fully local, zero cost — no API keys or subscriptions
19-tool MCP server with search, knowledge graph, and agent diaries
Temporal knowledge graph with validity windows
Write-ahead log audit trail for all mutations
cons: No cross-machine sync or multi-user support
Very new project — known bugs and corrected README claims
Experimental AAAK compression regresses retrieval quality
